title: buildah config --env takes only one envvar
body:
In a Dockerfile with a line like:
```
# isolate the filesystem with chroot.
ENV _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS="" BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
```
both environment variables are set as requested.  However, if you do

```
buildah config --env _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS="" BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
```
They are not.  I'm not sure if some kind of bash chicanery is going on here, but thought I'd raise the issue in case it's not and the config command needs to be changed to accept multiple arguments.  This is going on with Buildah v1.11.5.

comments.body:
You need to use the --env for each environment variable.
```
buildah config --env _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS="" --env BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
```
Yeah that does work, but I was hoping to see if we could do so without the second `--env` thrown in there.
Not really the way command line parsing works.

We could do something like
buildah config --env _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS="",BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot

But not sure this would work easily with command parsing and does not really match what you expected.
